steps.title: Find a difference between samples
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the two samples, so the sentence must highlight a difference rather than background or a shared result. The notes give direct differences: NiTi wire was 0.7 millimeter thick and rose 21.3 degrees Celsius, while EPDM rubber was 2 millimeters thick and rose 12 degrees Celsius.

steps.title: Rule out similarities and background
steps.content: A says both samples were studied, which groups them together instead of contrasting them. C defines torsional heating without mentioning the samples. D states a similarity, since both temperatures increased when twisted. The sentence comparing thickness gives a clear contrast between the two samples. The answer is B.
